description_general: Production stage covers raw material extraction, transport to manufacturing plant, and panel manufacturing. Includes timber sourcing (FSC-certified wood fibre), binder materials, steel frame components, and energy used in the production process. Packaging materials are also included (inkl. packaging).
description_a1: Raw material supply (A1) covers the extraction and processing of all input materials such as wood, glue , galvanised steel for sheet components.
description_a2: Transport of raw material to manufacturer (A2) covers the inbound logistics of all raw materials from their respective suppliers to the Lindner  manufacturing facility. This includes truck, rail and ship transport depending on the material origin.
description_a3: Lindner Manufacturing (A3) covers all production  processes such as chipboard cutting and profiling, steel sheet assembly ,finishing and packaging. Factory energy consumption  is included.

description_general: Transport of finished panels from the Lindner production facility to the construction site. Truck transport distance set default to 100km, scalable .
Mounting on site including installation kits and disposal of packaging waste.
Deconstruction of  panels from the current building, mostly mechanical. Depending on the future use of product, deconstruction can be done with or without damaging the product.
description_s1: Careful manual deconstruction to preserve panel integrity for refurbishment. Low energy input assumed.
description_s2: Mechanical removal using standard construction equipment. Panels are broken up for incineration transport.
description_s3: N/A

description_general: Transport of panels from the deconstruction site to the waste disposal facility, or Lindner in-case of future reuse.
description_s1: Transport to refurbishment workshop — assumed short distance within region.
description_s2: Transport to incineration facility — average regional distance.
description_s3: N/A

description_general: Waste processing operations such as incineration, recycling or refurbishment for a future reuse or neutralising the carbon intake in module A.
description_s1: Inspection, cleaning and minor repair of panels before reuse certification.
description_s2: Pre-processing of panels for feed into incineration furnace.
description_s3: N/A

description_general: Final waste dumping or neutralising the carbon intake in module A.
description_s1: Minimal residual waste to landfill — only panels failing refurbishment quality check.
description_s2: Ash residue from incineration disposed to landfill.
description_s3: N/A

description_general: Benefits and loads beyond the system boundary , credits from avoided primary production.
description_s1: Avoided production of new LIGNA panels credited at the same functional unit.
description_s2: Energy credits from electricity and heat recovered during incineration.
description_s3: N/A

description_general: Production stage covers raw material extraction and manufacturing of all components: concrete mix (cement, aggregates, water), steel reinforcement bars, and any surface treatment. Energy consumed at the Lindner production plant and all upstream supply chain processes are included (inkl. packaging).
description_a1: Raw material supply (A1) covers the extraction and processing of all input materials such as wood, glue , galvanised steel for sheet components.
description_a2: Transport of raw material to manufacturer (A2) covers the inbound logistics of all raw materials from their respective suppliers to the Lindner  manufacturing facility. This includes truck, rail and ship transport depending on the material origin.
description_a3: Lindner Manufacturing (A3) covers all production  processes such as chipboard cutting and profiling, steel sheet assembly ,finishing and packaging. Factory energy consumption  is included.

description_general: Transport of finished panels from the Lindner production facility to the construction site. Truck transport distance set default to 100km, scalable .
Mounting on site including installation kits and disposal of packaging waste.
Deconstruction of  panels from the current building, mostly mechanical. Depending on the future use of product, deconstruction can be done with or without damaging the product.
description_s1: Standard mechanical demolition — floor elements broken up with no intent to preserve geometry.
description_s2: Selective demolition using hydraulic tools to shred elements on site prior to transport.
description_s3: Careful mechanical lifting with vacuum equipment to maintain panel integrity for refurbishment.

description_general: Transport of panels from the deconstruction site to the waste disposal facility, or Lindner in-case of future reuse.
description_s1: Transport to landfill site — average 50 km.
description_s2: Transport to recycling facility — average 30 km.
description_s3: Transport to refurbishment facility — average 80 km.

description_general: Waste processing operations such as incineration, recycling or refurbishment for a future reuse or neutralising the carbon intake in module A.
description_s1: No processing — direct placement in landfill.
description_s2: Crushing and separation of concrete aggregate and steel reinforcement for recycling.
description_s3: Inspection, cleaning and structural assessment of each panel for reuse suitability.

description_general: Final waste dumping or neutralising the carbon intake in module A.
description_s1: 100% of material mass to landfill.
description_s2: Residual fines from crushing process to landfill — approximately 5% of mass.
description_s3: Panels failing quality inspection sent to landfill — assumed 10% rejection rate.

description_general: Benefits and loads beyond the system boundary , credits from avoided primary production.
description_s1: No credits — landfill scenario yields no material or energy recovery.
description_s2: Credits from recycled concrete aggregate (avoided primary aggregate) and recycled steel (avoided primary steel production).
description_s3: Credits from avoided production of new hollow floor elements at the same functional unit.

description_general: Production of LOOP raised floor panels including steel sheet forming, mineral fibre core, surface laminate, and edge trim. LOOP is designed for disassembly — the production process includes additional precision manufacturing steps to ensure panel reusability across multiple life cycles (inkl. packaging).
description_a1: Raw material supply (A1) covers the extraction and processing of all input materials such as wood, glue , galvanised steel for sheet components.
description_a2: Transport of raw material to manufacturer (A2) covers the inbound logistics of all raw materials from their respective suppliers to the Lindner  manufacturing facility. This includes truck, rail and ship transport depending on the material origin.
description_a3: Lindner Manufacturing (A3) covers all production  processes such as chipboard cutting and profiling, steel sheet assembly ,finishing and packaging. Factory energy consumption  is included.

description_general: Transport of finished panels from the Lindner production facility to the construction site. Truck transport distance set default to 100km, scalable .
Mounting on site including installation kits and disposal of packaging waste.
Deconstruction of  panels from the current building, mostly mechanical. Depending on the future use of product, deconstruction can be done with or without damaging the product.
description_s1: Systematic hand removal of panels — LOOP click-system allows tool-free disassembly. Very low energy input.
description_s2: Manual removal but with intent to send to landfill — panels are stacked for collection.
description_s3: Panels removed and stacked for shredding — mechanical equipment used.

description_general: Transport of panels from the deconstruction site to the waste disposal facility, or Lindner in-case of future reuse.
description_s1: Return transport to Lindner refurbishment centre via take-back logistics.
description_s2: Transport to landfill site.
description_s3: Transport to shredding and recycling facility.

description_general: Waste processing operations such as incineration, recycling or refurbishment for a future reuse or neutralising the carbon intake in module A.
description_s1: Inspection, surface cleaning and relamination where required. Panels graded for reuse quality level.
description_s2: No processing required before landfill.
description_s3: Mechanical shredding and material separation into mineral and metal fractions.

description_general: Final waste dumping or neutralising the carbon intake in module A.
description_s1: Panels failing Grade C quality threshold sent to landfill — assumed 5% rejection rate.
description_s2: 100% landfill disposal.
description_s3: Shredding residues and fines to landfill — approximately 8% of mass.

description_general: Benefits and loads beyond the system boundary , credits from avoided primary production.
description_s1: Full credit for avoided production of new LOOP panels — highest benefit scenario.
description_s2: Minimal credits — inert mineral materials in landfill yield no recovery benefit.
description_s3: Partial credits for recycled mineral aggregate and recovered steel fraction.

description_general: Raised floor panel production: steel sheet deep drawing, galvanising, mineral fibre filling, and understructure pedestal components. Includes upstream steel production emissions and energy use at the Lindner NORTEC facility (inkl. packaging).
description_a1: Raw material supply (A1) covers the extraction and processing of all input materials such as wood, glue , galvanised steel for sheet components.
description_a2: Transport of raw material to manufacturer (A2) covers the inbound logistics of all raw materials from their respective suppliers to the Lindner  manufacturing facility. This includes truck, rail and ship transport depending on the material origin.
description_a3: Lindner Manufacturing (A3) covers all production  processes such as chipboard cutting and profiling, steel sheet assembly ,finishing and packaging. Factory energy consumption  is included.

description_general: Transport of finished panels from the Lindner production facility to the construction site. Truck transport distance set default to 100km, scalable .
Mounting on site including installation kits and disposal of packaging waste.
Deconstruction of  panels from the current building, mostly mechanical. Depending on the future use of product, deconstruction can be done with or without damaging the product.
description_s1: Careful removal preserving panel geometry for reuse — tool-free disassembly.
description_s2: Standard demolition removal without care for panel condition — for landfill.
description_s3: Demolition removal — panels collected for recycling process.

description_general: Transport of panels from the deconstruction site to the waste disposal facility, or Lindner in-case of future reuse.
description_s1: Transport to reuse facility or directly to new building project — short assumed distance.
description_s2: Transport to landfill — standard regional distance.
description_s3: Transport to metals recycling facility.

description_general: Waste processing operations such as incineration, recycling or refurbishment for a future reuse or neutralising the carbon intake in module A.
description_s1: Quality inspection and cleaning — damaged panels separated from reusable stock.
description_s2: No pre-processing required for landfill disposal.
description_s3: Steel and mineral fibre separation — shredding and magnetic separation process.

description_general: Final waste dumping or neutralising the carbon intake in module A.
description_s1: Failed panels and manufacturing offcuts to landfill — small residual fraction.
description_s2: 100% of panel mass to landfill.
description_s3: Non-recoverable residues to landfill — approximately 3% of mass.

description_general: Benefits and loads beyond the system boundary , credits from avoided primary production.
description_s1: Avoided production credits for new NORTEC panels based on assumed reuse rate.
description_s2: Minimal credits — small steel recovery from landfill site assumed.
description_s3: Credits from avoided primary steel production based on recovered steel fraction.

description_general: Production of NORIT hollow floor screed boards: gypsum fibre composite manufacturing including natural gypsum sourcing, cellulose fibre processing, pressing and drying. Energy-intensive drying process is the dominant production impact (inkl. packaging).
description_a1: Raw material supply (A1) covers the extraction and processing of all input materials such as wood, glue , galvanised steel for sheet components.
description_a2: Transport of raw material to manufacturer (A2) covers the inbound logistics of all raw materials from their respective suppliers to the Lindner  manufacturing facility. This includes truck, rail and ship transport depending on the material origin.
description_a3: Lindner Manufacturing (A3) covers all production  processes such as chipboard cutting and profiling, steel sheet assembly ,finishing and packaging. Factory energy consumption  is included.

description_general: Transport of finished panels from the Lindner production facility to the construction site. Truck transport distance set default to 100km, scalable .
Mounting on site including installation kits and disposal of packaging waste.
Deconstruction of  panels from the current building, mostly mechanical. Depending on the future use of product, deconstruction can be done with or without damaging the product.
description_s1: Mechanical removal using scraping tools — boards broken in place.
description_s2: Mechanical removal — fragments collected for landfill transport.
description_s3: Selective removal to maximise intact board yield for shredding.

description_general: Transport of panels from the deconstruction site to the waste disposal facility, or Lindner in-case of future reuse.
description_s1: Transport to landfill — assumed 40 km average.
description_s2: Transport to landfill — assumed 40 km average.
description_s3: Transport to gypsum recycling facility — assumed 60 km.

description_general: Waste processing operations such as incineration, recycling or refurbishment for a future reuse or neutralising the carbon intake in module A.
description_s1: No processing — broken board material directly to landfill.
description_s2: No processing — direct landfill.
description_s3: Shredding and separation to recover clean gypsum fraction for recycling into new gypsum products.

description_general: Final waste dumping or neutralising the carbon intake in module A.
description_s1: 100% of board material to landfill.
description_s2: 100% of board material to landfill.
description_s3: Non-recyclable impurities and cellulose fraction to landfill — approximately 15% of mass.

description_general: Benefits and loads beyond the system boundary , credits from avoided primary production.
description_s1: No recovery — no Module D credits for landfill scenario.
description_s2: No recovery — no Module D credits for landfill scenario.
description_s3: Credits from avoided primary gypsum production based on recovered recycled gypsum fraction replacing natural gypsum.
